title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 April 2024.

items.title: **Garry's Mod** Steam Workshop Hit By Nintendo Takedown Demand
bullets: In the post on Steam, Facepunch Studios urged players and creators to help them remove every trace of anything Nintendo-themed from Garry's Mod.

It was originally thought that the DMCA requests coming from 'Nintendo' were false and were being circulated by a malicious operator, but Facepunch Studios confirmed that the requests are legit and come directly from the legal team at Nintendo.
Many users are now wondering what took Nintendo so long to make this formal request.

items.title: **Kazuma Kaneko**, longtime **Atlus** artist, departs after 35-year run
bullets: During his Atlus tenure, Kaneko touched everything from Persona to Shin Megami Tensei and Soul Hackers to even Devil May Cry.
The artist has jumped ship over to COLOPL.

items.title: **Steam **amends **refund **policy, nixing advanced access loophole in the process
bullets: In the past, the "refund timer" would reset on an early access game's full release, allowing players with hundreds of hours to get an automatic refund when a game moved to full release.

items.title: **Suspect **charged for cancelling **Nintendo **Japan events by threatening to 'kill everyone' for 'shitty games'
**Switch Online**'s N64 app gets two more games today
Previously thought to be only over one million players in a single day on **Fallout 76**, there has been almost five million players across all Fallout games in a single day
bullets: The two events will co-locate to create 'the biggest pop culture weekend in the UK'
Normally GamesIndustry Biz supports EGX with the business offering (Meetings with investors .etc)
They have hinted that their offering will also be part of the EGX-MCM event.

items.title: **CI Games **hits record fiscal revenue in 2023
bullets: CI Games has generated over $60 million in consolidated net revenue for its fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The figure represents a 400% year-over-year growth, the highest in the company's history.
CI Games attributed 83% of its sales revenue to Lords of the Fallen, launched October 13, 2023.
Meanwhile, 17% was generated from its back catalog of titles, and Sniper Ghost Warrior Contracts 2 accounted for the remaining 9% of revenue.
